hypothesis: If tensor completion surrogate models (CPD-S with ranks 1/2/4; NeAT with ranks 24/32; and ensembles) are used to predict Young's modulus E from lattice design parameters under both uniform random and biased sampling, then they will achieve higher R² scores than baseline ML methods (Gaussian Process, XGBoost, MLP) across training sizes and will exhibit less performance degradation under biased sampling because they exploit the inherent multiway structure of the design tensor.
